runs.text: The Man Who Built Hotmail Tells His Journey | Ft Sabeer Bhatia

accessibilityData.label: The Man Who Built Hotmail Tells His Journey | Ft Sabeer Bhatia 57 minutes

runs.text:
What does it take to build a product used by millions around the world? In this episode of the Zahra Jani Show, we sit down with Sabeer Bhatia, the co-founder of Hotmail, to explore the journey behind one of the internet’s most iconic success stories, from leaving Apple to building Hotmail and eventually selling it to Microsoft.

Beyond the Hotmail story, we discuss innovation, risk-taking, the difference between Indian and Silicon Valley startup culture, the Indian education system, AI, Aadhaar, Steve Jobs, Bill Gates, and why original thinking matters more than ever in today’s world.

If you enjoyed this conversation, don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe for more insightful episodes.

Chapters
- Bill Gates & The Microsoft Hotmail Deal
- Why India Has No Google or Nvidia
- Where Are The Next Big Opportunities?

- Thanks for watching!

About the guest 

Today’s guest is Sabeer Bhatia, the entrepreneur behind one of the internet’s most iconic innovations. Best known for building Hotmail and closing a landmark deal with Microsoft, he continues to challenge conventional thinking around startups, innovation, and the future of technology.

--------
About the show:
Zahra Jani Show is all about conversations that bring real stories, ideas, and experiences from everyday life. Through every episode, we discover new things, learn lessons, and expand our perspectives. 

So, sit back with your favorite drink or snack and enjoy the show. Don’t forget to like, share & subscribe!

New Episodes - Every Tuesday at 7 PM

📩 For business inquiries: zahrabmnb@gmail.com

Disclaimer: This video is intended solely for educational purposes and opinions shared by the guests are his/her personal views. We do not seek to defame or harm any person/brand/product mentioned in the video. Our goal is to provide information to help audience make informed choices.
